element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns coordinate system and grid navigation
Player positions fleet before combat begins
Player learns hit/miss feedback and develops strategic targeting
Player adapts to opponent behavior and evasion tactics

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based alternating tension with episodic relief tied to successful hits and ship eliminations

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden enemy ship placement creates fog-of-war deduction puzzle

difficulty_curve.early_game: Player learns grid targeting and ship placement fundamentals
difficulty_curve.mid_game: AI opponent targeting becomes more sophisticated; player must balance offense and defense
difficulty_curve.late_game: Endgame scenarios with reduced ship counts increase tension and consequence weight
notable_spikes: Transition from placement phase to active combat
When opponent sinks player's first ship
Final ship scenarios where single hit determines outcome

notable_level_patterns.description: Player fires shot → observes hit/miss feedback → updates mental model of ship locations → makes informed next shot
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Core gameplay mechanic across all matches

notable_level_patterns.description: Player has complete knowledge of own fleet but zero knowledge of opponent fleet; success depends on probabilistic inference and pattern recognition

sources_used: wikipedia (Battleship disambiguation page - confirms 1993 NES release by Mindscape and puzzle/game classification)
extraction_notes: Source material is minimal disambiguation content. Detailed game mechanics, level structures, and pacing data not available in provided source. Output reflects general Battleship game structure based on paradigm classification rather than specific documentation of the 1993 NES version.
